---
title: "Veille arXiv"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Veille arXiv

## Catégories surveillées

| Catégorie arXiv | Description | Fréquence de scan |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `cs.AI` | Artificial Intelligence | Quotidien |
| `cs.CL` | Computation and Language (NLP) | Quotidien |
| `cs.LG` | Machine Learning | Quotidien |
| `cs.MA` | Multi-Agent Systems | Quotidien |
| `cs.SE` | Software Engineering | Quotidien |
| `stat.ML` | Machine Learning (Stats) | Quotidien |

## Filtres de pertinence

Le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` filtre les papers selon :

| Filtre | Mots-clés | Action |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Agentic | `agent`, `agentic`, `autonomous`, `tool-use` | Priorité haute |
| LLM | `LLM`, `language model`, `transformer`, `GPT`, `Claude` | Priorité haute |
| Sandbox | `sandbox`, `isolation`, `container`, `microVM` | Priorité moyenne |
| RAG | `RAG`, `retrieval augmented`, `GraphRAG`, `knowledge graph` | Priorité haute |
| MCP | `MCP`, `model context protocol` | Priorité haute |
| Harness | `code agent`, `IDE`, `coding assistant` | Priorité moyenne |
| Benchmark | `benchmark`, `evaluation`, `leaderboard` | Priorité moyenne |

## Format de sortie

Chaque paper détecté génère une entrée dans `content/veille/arxiv-feed.qmd` :

```markdown
### [Titre du paper]
- **arXiv** : 2507.17311
- **Date** : 2025-07-23
- **Auteurs** : Intern-AI et al.
- **Catégorie** : cs.AI
- **Tags** : agentic, climate, research
- **Résumé** : Premier self-evolving AI agent pour climate science...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17311
```

## Derniers papers détectés

::: callout-note
## Section auto-générée
Cette section est automatiquement mise à jour par le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` via GitHub Actions. Les entrées ci-dessous sont générées à chaque exécution.
:::

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_START -->
### LLM-Powered Agentic AI for 5G/6G Networks: A Tutorial and Survey on Architectures, Protocols, and Standardization
- **arXiv** : 2607.16066v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Mazene Ameur, Abdelkader Mekrache, Bouziane Brik, Adlen Ksentini
- **Score** : 16
- **Résumé** : Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI), enabled by Large Language Models, marks a shift from rule-based automation toward autonomous, goal-driven control of Next-Generation Networks (NGNs). Existing surveys treat the two domains in isolation, leaving protocol integration, evaluation, and standardizati...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16066v1

### Frontier AI performance across the business disciplines: a case-grounded benchmark of knowledge work and analytical reasoning
- **arXiv** : 2607.16057v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Ajay Patel, Kartik Hosanagar, Ramayya Krishnan, Chris Callison-Burch, Karim Lakhani
- **Score** : 16
- **Résumé** : Large language models (LLMs) are improving rapidly as reflected in benchmark scores, yet these AI benchmarks largely test capabilities such as factual recall, narrow question answering, mathematical problem-solving, and coding and agentic tool-use. What remains poorly measured is AI progress on the ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16057v1

### PagedWeight: Efficient MoE LLM Serving with Dynamic Quality-Aware Weight Quantization
- **arXiv** : 2607.16184v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Yuchen Yang, Yifan Zhao, Anisha Dasgupta, Sasa Misailovic
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is a popular class of large language models (LLMs), offering high efficiency and accuracy. However, in KV-cache-intensive serving scenarios, MoEs often exhibit a tension between the GPU memory requirements of the model weights and the growing KV cache. We propose PagedWeight...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16184v1

### When Does Muon Help Agentic Reinforcement Learning?
- **arXiv** : 2607.16169v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Kai Ruan, Jinghao Lin, Zihe Huang, Ziqi Zhou, Qianshan Wei
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Muon is competitive with AdamW in large-scale pre-training, but its value for reinforcement-learning (RL) post-training remains unclear. We study vanilla Muon in sparse-reward agentic RL through matched single-seed comparisons with AdamW on ALFWorld using Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct. Under Group-in-Group ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16169v1

### Evaluating Open-Weight LLMs for Generating Structured Threat Information for Autonomous Vehicle Vulnerabilities
- **arXiv** : 2607.16175v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Md Erfan, Ahmed Ryan, Md Kamal Hossain Chowdhury, Md Rayhanur Rahman
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs) rely on interconnected software and hardware components, including sensors, Electronic Control Units, in-vehicle infotainment systems, and telematics units, where vulnerabilities can compromise assets, users, and vehicle operations. These vulnerabilities are ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16175v1

### When Do Multi-Agent Systems Help? An Information Bottleneck Perspective
- **arXiv** : 2607.16133v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Wendi Yu, Lianhao Zhou, Xiangjue Dong, Sai Sudarshan Barath, Declan Staunton
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : LLM powered multi-agent systems (MAS) have emerged as a promising paradigm for complex tasks. However, their advantages over single-agent systems (SAS) remain unclear, with performance varying inconsistently across settings. Here, we provide an information bottleneck perspective on elucidating the d...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16133v1

### The Honest Quorum Problem: Epistemic Byzantine Fault Tolerance for Agentic Infrastructure
- **arXiv** : 2607.16109v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Jun He, Deying Yu
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : State machine replication (SMR) and Byzantine fault-tolerant (BFT) consensus guarantee agreement despite a bounded number of arbitrary, colluding faulty participants. However, these guarantees rely on participants outside this set correctly executing the protocol's transition semantics. Agentic vali...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16109v1

### Understanding Reasoning from Pretraining to Post-Training
- **arXiv** : 2607.16097v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Jingyan Shen, Ang Li, Salman Rahman, Yifan Sun, Micah Goldblum
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Reinforcement learning (RL) has become central to improving large language models (LLMs) on complex reasoning tasks, yet RL post-training is largely studied in isolation from the pretraining that precedes it. As a result, two basic questions remain open: (1) how do pretraining choices (model size, d...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16097v1

### What Does It Take to Research with AI? A Rapid Review of Competencies to Train LLM-Literate Researchers
- **arXiv** : 2607.16083v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Danilo Monteiro Ribeiro, Ronnie de Souza Santos, Rodrigo Siqueira, Breno Andrade, Rafael Batista Duarte
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : The growing adoption of Large Language Models in scientific research has created a need to understand what competencies researchers and graduate students require to use these tools critically and responsibly. This rapid review analyzed 194 articles retrieved from Elicit and Google Scholar (2022 to 2...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16083v1

### Frontier Language Models Struggle to Copy: Text Can Be Better Viewed in 2D
- **arXiv** : 2607.16072v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Haodong Wen, Yiran Zhang, Yingfa Chen, Kaifeng Lyu
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : While large language models (LLMs) can solve advanced reasoning problems in seconds, we show that even frontier models fail to perform a much simpler operation: exactly copying an input string that lies well within their context windows. We attribute this failure to positional encodings in Transform...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16072v1

### CRAFT: Clustering Rubrics to Diagnose Weak LLM Capabilities and Generate Targeted Fine-Tuning Data
- **arXiv** : 2607.16122v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Vipul Gupta, Zihao Wang, Razvan-Gabriel Dumitru, MohammadHossein Rezaei, Aakash Sabharwal
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Evaluations should do more than measure a models current performance. They should tell us what to fix for the next model iteration and provide a way to generate targeted post training data. Most evaluation pipelines identify weak examples, topics, or categories, but they leave the underlying capabil...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16122v1

### AI Watermark Evidence Fails Forensic Readiness: An Empirical Evaluation
- **arXiv** : 2607.16010v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Saifur Rahman Tamim, Amir Labib Khan
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Governments are increasingly mandating that LLM-generated content carry watermarks. The EU AI Act calls for markings that are "sufficiently reliable and robust." California's SB 942 requires disclosure that is "permanent or extraordinarily difficult to remove." Both mandates rest on an untested assu...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16010v1

### A Blueprint for Equilibrium-Based Differentiable Continuous-Variable Thermodynamic Computing
- **arXiv** : 2607.16183v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Owen Lockwood, Jérémy Béjanin, Joost Bus, Christopher Chamberland, Patrick Huembeli
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : To address the escalating energy and latency demands of machine-learning workloads, we introduce a blueprint for an energy-efficient and fast thermodynamic computing stack that leverages stochastic analog processes in physical hardware. In this work, we focus on energy-based thermodynamic computing ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16183v1

### An Exam for Active Observers
- **arXiv** : 2607.16165v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Jiarui Zhang, Muzi Tao, Shangshang Wang, Ollie Liu, Xuezhe Ma
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Human vision is a closed loop: gaze is continuously redirected by intermediate hypotheses rather than a single snapshot. Decades of psychophysics and cognitive science have argued that this active observation is essential for a wide range of tasks. Whether today's multimodal large language models (M...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16165v1

### PRISA: Proactive Infrastructure LiDAR Framework for Intersection Safety Assessment
- **arXiv** : 2607.16156v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Tam Bang, Hussam Abubakr, Emiliano de la Garza Villarreal, Truc Phuong Nguyen, Austin Harris
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Urban intersections are among the most hazardous locations in road networks, posing significant risks to vehicles and vulnerable road users (VRUs) such as pedestrians and cyclists. The complexity of multi-agent interactions demands continuous, real-time monitoring systems capable of anticipating con...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16156v1

### Toward Semantic Communication for Real-time Mobile 3D Reconstruction
- **arXiv** : 2607.16128v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Fangzhou Zhao, Yao Sun, Xuesong Liu, Runze Cheng, Shang Kai
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Real-time mobile 3D reconstruction is fundamental to many emerging applications such as autonomous navigation and digital twin construction, where a moving platform continuously captures an image stream and transmit to a computing server for scene understanding. Unlike offline reconstruction, camera...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16128v1

### CADAQUES: A Cost-Aware Dual Architecture for Query-Efficient Autonomous Discovery
- **arXiv** : 2607.16127v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Jorge Bravo-Abad
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Autonomous discovery systems couple a resource that answers queries (a simulator, instrument, or analytic model) to an algorithm that selects what to query next. Most software frameworks for this loop inherit the control structure of numerical optimization: campaigns run for a fixed number of iterat...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16127v1

### Rate-Utility Frontiers for Language Encodings: Comparing Tokens, Bytes, and Pixels Under Controlled Linguistic Content
- **arXiv** : 2607.16117v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Ingo Ziegler, Martin Krebs, Desmond Elliott
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Language models encode text as subword tokens, raw bytes, or rendered pixels, but these encodings are usually compared under modeling constraints that expose different amounts of linguistic content to models across different languages. We instead ask what each encoding preserves when both the conten...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16117v1

### When Model Merging Rivals Joint Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning: A Task-Vector Geometry Analysis
- **arXiv** : 2607.16062v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : S. Aaron McClendon
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Model merging is promoted as a substitute for joint multi-task training, yet in the reinforcement-learning setting this substitution is essentially never tested against the baseline it claims to replace: methods merge independently released agents precisely because a joint model is unavailable. We b...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16062v1

### DiffTestGen: Change-Directed LLM-Based Testing for Exposing Behavioral Differences
- **arXiv** : 2607.16024v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-17
- **Auteurs** : Huimin Hu, Cristian Cadar, Michael Pradel
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : As software evolves over time, it is important to ensure that any behavioral changes occur as intended by developers. A promising approach for this goal is to generate tests that expose behavioral differences between the old and new versions of a program. However, current approaches fail to trigger ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.16024v1

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_END -->
